Disambiguation via Prosody in Cypriot Turkish: An LFG Implementation Statistical Analysis

# ================================
# loading necessary libraries
# ================================
library(lme4) # Linear Mixed-Effects Models
library(lmerTest) # for Satterthwaite p-values
library(emmeans) # for post-hoc analysis
library(ggplot2) # data visualization
library(dplyr) # data cleaning
library(tidyr) # data manipulation
library(sjPlot) # model tables & diagnostics
library(performance) # model validation (check_model)
library(broom.mixed) 
library(kableExtra)   # table format
library(qqplotr)
# ================================
# data loading and cleaning
# ================================
# reading the data (leave if it is ";", change if it is ",")
data <- read.csv("thesis_data_1.csv", sep = ";", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# cleaning the data
clean_base <- data %>%
  filter(grepl("^p\\d+_s\\d+_", file_name)) %>%
  separate(file_name, into = c("participant", "item", "condition"), sep = "_") %>%
  mutate(across(c(condition, participant, item), as.factor)) %>%
  # the formula:  note: this formula is a semitone scale with a 100Hz reference.
  mutate(pitch_st = 12 * log(pitch_hz / 100) / log(2))

# leaving out the problematic 7 recordings
exclude_list <- c("p10_s07_decl", "p10_s09_decl", "p10_s15_decl", 
                  "p18_s09_polQ", "p1_s05_decl", "p1_s15_decl", 
                  "p9_s07_decl") 

data_final <- clean_base %>%
  filter(!(interaction(participant, item, condition, sep = "_") %in% exclude_list))

# ==============================================================================
# splitting the data
# ==============================================================================

# for pitch analysis (p1, p2, p3)
# the target output: 293 recordings * 3 points = 879
pitch_data <- data_final %>%
  filter(label %in% c("p1", "p2", "p3")) %>%
  mutate(label = factor(label, levels = c("p1", "p2", "p3")))

# for duration analysis (s1, s2, s3)
# the target output: 293 recordings * 3 points = 879
dur_data <- data_final %>%
  filter(label %in% c("s1", "s2", "s3")) %>%
  mutate(label = factor(label, levels = c("s1", "s2", "s3")))

# ==============================================================================
# checking
# ==============================================================================
cat("Final Pitch Data Rows:", nrow(pitch_data), "\n") # 879 
Final Pitch Data Rows: 879 
cat("Final Duration Data Rows:", nrow(dur_data), "\n") # 879 
Final Duration Data Rows: 879 
# seeing how many left for the anlaysis
pitch_data %>%
  group_by(condition) %>%
  summarise(n_sentences = n() / 3)
# A tibble: 2 × 2
  condition n_sentences
  <fct>           <dbl>
1 decl              144
2 polQ              149
# ==============================================================================
# descriptive statistics for pitch (semitones & hertz)
# ==============================================================================

pitch_summary_table <- pitch_data %>%
  # grouping the data
  group_by(condition, label) %>%
  # calculating
  summarise(
    # Semitone ´
    Mean_ST = round(mean(pitch_st, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    SD_ST   = round(sd(pitch_st, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    
    # Hertz (for readers)
    Mean_Hz = round(mean(pitch_hz, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    SD_Hz   = round(sd(pitch_hz, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    

    N = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  rename(
    "Sentence Type" = condition,
    "Point"         = label,
    "M (SemiT)"        = Mean_ST,
    "SD (SemiT)"       = SD_ST,
    "M (Hz)"        = Mean_Hz,
    "SD (Hz)"       = SD_Hz
  ) %>%
  # creating the table
  kable(caption = "Table X. Descriptive Statistics of Pitch Values (Semitone vs. Hz)", 
        booktabs = TRUE, 
        align = "c") %>%
  kable_classic(full_width = F, html_font = "Times New Roman") %>%
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top") %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE)

# printing the table
pitch_summary_table
Table X. Descriptive Statistics of Pitch Values (Semitone vs. Hz)
Sentence Type Point M (SemiT) SD (SemiT) M (Hz) SD (Hz) N
decl p1 13.31 4.17 221.9 51.8 144
p2 10.55 3.50 187.7 38.0 144
p3 8.78 3.24 168.9 30.7 144
polQ p1 14.10 2.33 227.8 31.4 149
p2 12.22 2.99 205.7 39.4 149
p3 22.48 3.06 371.8 60.3 149 
# ==============================================================================
# descriptive statistics for duration
# ==============================================================================
dur_summary <- dur_data %>%
  group_by(condition, label) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_Dur = round(mean(duration, na.rm = TRUE), 3), 
    SD_Dur = round(sd(duration, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    N = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  rename(
    "Sentence Type" = condition,
    "Syllable" = label,
    "M (sec)" = Mean_Dur,
    "SD (sec)" = SD_Dur
  ) %>%
  kable(caption = "Table X. Descriptive Statistics of Syllable Duration (Seconds)", 
        booktabs = TRUE, align = "c") %>%
  kable_classic(full_width = F, html_font = "Times New Roman") %>%
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top") 

dur_summary
Table X. Descriptive Statistics of Syllable Duration (Seconds)
Sentence Type Syllable M (sec) SD (sec) N
decl s1 0.177 0.065 144
s2 0.175 0.052 144
s3 0.228 0.046 144
polQ s1 0.174 0.064 149
s2 0.173 0.052 149
s3 0.279 0.056 149 
# ==============================================================================
# LMER modelling
# ==============================================================================
# Pitch Model: Interaction between Condition and Measurement Point
pitch_model <- lmer(pitch_st ~ condition * label + (1 | participant) + (1 | item), 
                    data = pitch_data)

# Duration Model: Interaction between Condition and Syllable Position
dur_model <- lmer(duration ~ condition * label + (1 | participant) + (1 | item), 
                  data = dur_data)
# ==============================================================================
# anova results
# ==============================================================================
print(anova(pitch_model))
Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
                Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
condition       6094.4  6094.4     1 841.51  963.13 < 2.2e-16 ***
label           2651.9  1325.9     2 839.67  209.54 < 2.2e-16 ***
condition:label 7628.6  3814.3     2 839.67  602.79 < 2.2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
print(anova(dur_model))
Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
                 Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
condition       0.05363 0.05363     1 842.84  21.514 4.071e-06 ***
label           1.21077 0.60539     2 839.85 242.862 < 2.2e-16 ***
condition:label 0.13459 0.06729     2 839.85  26.996 4.338e-12 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
# ==============================================================================
# APA style anova table
# ==============================================================================
# matching the numbers here exactly with the anova results.
anova_results <- data.frame(
  Source = c("Condition", "Label", "Condition : Label", 
             "Condition", "Label", "Condition : Label"),
  SS = c(6094.4, 2651.9, 7628.6, 0.054, 1.211, 0.135),
  df = c(1, 2, 2, 1, 2, 2),
  F_value = c(963.13, 209.54, 602.79, 21.51, 242.86, 27.00),
  p_value = c("< .001", "< .001", "< .001", "< .001", "< .001", "< .001")
)

# creating the table
anova_results %>%
  kbl(caption = "Table X. Type III ANOVA Results for Pitch and Duration Models",
      col.names = c("Source", "Sum of Sq", "df", "F", "p"),
      align = "lcccc",
      booktabs = TRUE) %>%
  kable_classic(full_width = F, html_font = "Times New Roman") %>%
  # adding a heading to separate the Pitch and Duration sections.
  pack_rows("Pitch Analysis (Semitones)", 1, 3) %>%
  pack_rows("Duration Analysis (Seconds)", 4, 6) %>%
  # making bold the important line (Interaction)
  row_spec(c(3, 6), bold = T, color = "black") %>%
  # adding footnote
  footnote(general = "Degrees of freedom estimated via Satterthwaite's method. Significance: *** p < .001.")
Table X. Type III ANOVA Results for Pitch and Duration Models
Source Sum of Sq df F p
Pitch Analysis (Semitones)
Condition 6094.400 1 963.13 < .001
Label 2651.900 2 209.54 < .001
Condition : Label 7628.600 2 602.79 < .001
Duration Analysis (Seconds)
Condition 0.054 1 21.51 < .001
Label 1.211 2 242.86 < .001
Condition : Label 0.135 2 27.00 < .001
Note:
Degrees of freedom estimated via Satterthwaite's method. Significance: *** p < .001. 
# ==============================================================================
# Post-hoc analysis
# ==============================================================================
pitch_emm <- emmeans(pitch_model, pairwise ~ condition | label)
dur_emm <- emmeans(dur_model, pairwise ~ condition | label)

print(pitch_emm$contrasts)
label = p1:
 contrast    estimate    SE  df t.ratio p.value
 decl - polQ   -0.699 0.295 841  -2.373  0.0179

label = p2:
 contrast    estimate    SE  df t.ratio p.value
 decl - polQ   -1.581 0.295 841  -5.366 <0.0001

label = p3:
 contrast    estimate    SE  df t.ratio p.value
 decl - polQ  -13.617 0.295 841 -46.228 <0.0001

Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger 
print(dur_emm$contrasts)
label = s1:
 contrast     estimate      SE  df t.ratio p.value
 decl - polQ  0.002764 0.00585 841   0.473  0.6364

label = s2:
 contrast     estimate      SE  df t.ratio p.value
 decl - polQ  0.000795 0.00585 841   0.136  0.8918

label = s3:
 contrast     estimate      SE  df t.ratio p.value
 decl - polQ -0.050698 0.00585 841  -8.672 <0.0001

Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger 
# ==============================================================================
# POST-HOC analysis table
# ==============================================================================
# getting the values 
posthoc_df <- data.frame(
  Label = c("p1", "p2", "p3", "s1", "s2", "s3"),
  Contrast = rep("Decl - PolQ", 6),
  Estimate = c(-0.70, -1.58, -13.62, 0.003, 0.001, -0.051),
  SE = c(0.30, 0.30, 0.30, 0.006, 0.006, 0.006),
  t_ratio = c(-2.37, -5.37, -46.23, 0.47, 0.14, -8.67),
  p_value = c(".018*", "< .001***", "< .001***", ".636", ".892", "< .001***")
)

# creating the table
posthoc_df %>%
  kbl(caption = "Table Y. Pairwise Comparisons of Pitch and Duration at Each Point",
      col.names = c("Measurement Point", "Contrast", "Estimate (Diff)", "SE", "t-ratio", "p-value"),
      align = "lccccc",
      booktabs = TRUE) %>%
  kable_material(c("striped", "hover"), full_width = F, html_font = "Times New Roman") %>%
  # grouping (Pitch vs Duration)
  pack_rows("Pitch Analysis (p1-p3 in Semitones)", 1, 3) %>%
  pack_rows("Duration Analysis (s1-s3 in Seconds)", 4, 6) %>%
  # making bold the important lines (p3 ve s3)
  row_spec(c(3, 6), bold = T, background = "#f2f2f2") %>%
  # footnote
  footnote(general = "Negative estimates indicate higher values for Polar Questions. p-values are adjusted for multiple comparisons.")
Table Y. Pairwise Comparisons of Pitch and Duration at Each Point
Measurement Point Contrast Estimate (Diff) SE t-ratio p-value
Pitch Analysis (p1-p3 in Semitones)
p1 Decl - PolQ -0.700 0.300 -2.37 .018*
p2 Decl - PolQ -1.580 0.300 -5.37 < .001***
p3 Decl - PolQ -13.620 0.300 -46.23 < .001***
Duration Analysis (s1-s3 in Seconds)
s1 Decl - PolQ 0.003 0.006 0.47 .636
s2 Decl - PolQ 0.001 0.006 0.14 .892
s3 Decl - PolQ -0.051 0.006 -8.67 < .001***
Note:
Negative estimates indicate higher values for Polar Questions. p-values are adjusted for multiple comparisons. 
# ==============================================================================
# model summary
# ==============================================================================
# This table displays the fixed effects (estimates), significance levels (p), 
# and overall model fit (R-squared) for both Pitch and Duration models.
tab_model(pitch_model, dur_model, 
          dv.labels = c("Pitch (Semitone)", "Duration (Sec)"), # outcome variables
          show.stat = TRUE, show.df = TRUE) # displays t-statistics for coefficient strength and shows Satterthwaite-approximated degrees of freedom
  Pitch (Semitone) Duration (Sec)

label [p2] -2.76 -3.34 – -2.18 -9.31 <0.001 870.00

[p2]		 0.88 0.07 – 1.70 2.12 0.034 870.00

# ==============================================================================
# model performance plots
# ==============================================================================
# model-data fit
plot(check_predictions(pitch_model)) + theme_bw()
# normal distribution of errors
plot(check_normality(pitch_model)) + theme_bw()
# homogeneity of variance
plot(check_heteroscedasticity(pitch_model)) + theme_bw()
# ==============================================================================
# visualisation: pitch contours
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# This script automatically extracts predicted values from the model,
# ensuring the plot stays synchronized with the statistical results.
# ==============================================================================
# adjusting 'specs' and 'by' to match your model structure
pitch_emms <- emmeans(pitch_model, specs = ~ condition * label) %>% 
              as.data.frame() # Convert to a format ggplot understands

# generating the plot using the dynamic 'pitch_emms' object
ggplot(pitch_emms, aes(x = label, y = emmean, group = condition, color = condition)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) + 
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = emmean - 1.96*SE, ymax = emmean + 1.96*SE), width = 0.1) +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  facet_wrap(~condition, labeller = as_labeller(c("decl" = "Declarative", 
                                                 "polQ" = "Polar Question"))) + 
  theme_bw() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#2C3E50", "#b5332a")) + 
  labs(title = "Estimated Pitch Contours",
       x = "Measurement Point", 
       y = "Estimated Pitch (Semitones)") +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray95"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12))
# ==============================================================================
# visualisation: duration
# ==============================================================================
# converting the values predicted from the model (marginal effects) into a data frame
pred_dur <- get_model_data(dur_model, type = "pred", terms = c("label", "condition"))

# plotting the duration difference
ggplot(pred_dur, aes(x = x, y = predicted, fill = group)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), 
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.2) +
  # making it horizontal
  coord_flip() + 
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#4A6984", "#A3C1AD"), 
                    labels = c("Declarative", "Polar Question")) +
  labs(title = "Syllable Duration Prediction: Syllable Durations",
       subtitle = "Model-based length comparison (X-axis represents duration in sec)",
       x = "Syllables (s1, s2, s3)",
       y = "Predicted Duration (seconds)",
       fill = "Sentence Type") +
  theme(legend.position = "top")
